description: Replace fragile CSS class selectors (.bg-success, .border-border, .bg-accent) in side panel E2E tests with data-testid attributes to eliminate false positives from vacuous assertions and brittleness from Tailwind class changes.

userStories.title: Add data-testid attributes to side panel React components for E2E test selectors
userStories.description: As a test author, I want side panel components to have stable data-testid attributes so that E2E tests can locate elements reliably without depending on Tailwind CSS class names.
acceptanceCriteria: The plugin icon container element has a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='plugin-icon-container')
The status dot element indicating plugin ready state has a data-testid (e.g., data-testid='plugin-status-dot')
The plugin card element has a data-testid that includes the plugin name (e.g., data-testid='plugin-card-{pluginName}')
The connection status indicator has a data-testid (e.g., data-testid='connection-status')
The loading/spinner element has a data-testid (e.g., data-testid='loading-indicator')
The tool invocation animation element has a data-testid (e.g., data-testid='tool-invocation-indicator')
userStories.notes:
This story adds data-testid attributes to the side panel React components. The exact component file paths need to be discovered by searching the codebase. Start by looking in:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/
- Search for the PluginIcon or PluginCard component
- Search for 'bg-success' to find the status dot element
- Search for 'border-border' to find the icon container
- Search for 'bg-accent' to find the connection status indicator
- Search for loading/spinner components

Add data-testid attributes to these elements. The attributes should be stable identifiers that don't change with styling. Where the plugin name is relevant (e.g., plugin cards), include it dynamically: data-testid={`plugin-card-${plugin.name}`}.

Do NOT change any existing CSS classes or styling. Only add data-testid attributes.

userStories.title: Update side-panel-icons.e2e.ts to use data-testid selectors and fix assertion ordering
userStories.description: As a test author, I want icon tests to use data-testid selectors and capture active icon HTML after the status dot is confirmed visible so that tests don't produce false positives from wrong-element matching.
acceptanceCriteria: All .border-border selectors are replaced with [data-testid='plugin-icon-container'] or equivalent data-testid selector
All .bg-success selectors are replaced with [data-testid='plugin-status-dot'] or equivalent
The .first() calls are removed or replaced with more specific scoping (e.g., within a plugin card located by text)
In the 'inactive icon differs from active icon' test, activeHtml is captured AFTER the status dot is confirmed visible (move the HTML capture after the toBeVisible() assertion)
The svgCount assertion uses expect(locator).toHaveCount(0) with auto-waiting instead of .count() followed by manual expect
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/side-panel-icons.e2e.ts

Fragile selectors:
- Lines 90, 126, 166, 231: .border-border used to find icon container. Replace with data-testid.
- Lines 131, 171: .bg-success used for status dot. Replace with data-testid.
- Lines 126, 166: .first() selects first matching element on page -- could be wrong element. Instead, first locate the plugin card by text (e.g., sidePanelPage.getByText('E2E Test').locator('..')) then find the icon container within it.

Capture ordering (lines 161-174): The test captures activeHtml at line 167, then checks .bg-success at line 171. If the HTML is captured before the icon state updates, activeHtml may still show inactive state. Fix: check the status dot first, then capture the HTML.

svgCount (lines 237-238): Replace:
  const svgCount = await avatarContainer.locator('svg').count();
  expect(svgCount).toBe(0);
With:
  await expect(avatarContainer.locator('svg')).toHaveCount(0);

This uses Playwright's auto-retry, which waits for the condition to be stable.

userStories.title: Update side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts to use data-testid selectors and fix loader timing
userStories.description: As a test author, I want data flow tests to use data-testid selectors so that toBeHidden() assertions don't pass vacuously when CSS classes are renamed.
acceptanceCriteria: All .bg-accent/20 selectors are replaced with data-testid selectors for connection status
All .bg-success selectors are replaced with data-testid selectors for status dot
All .bg-primary.rounded-full selectors are replaced with data-testid selectors for tool invocation indicator
The loader visibility test (around lines 419-432) uses a longer slow mode or polls for the loader to appear, instead of relying on a fixed 3-second window
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts

Fragile CSS selectors:
- Lines 124, 194, 300: .bg-accent/20 used for connection status indicator. If the Tailwind class is renamed, toBeHidden()/toBeVisible() passes vacuously because the locator matches nothing.
- Lines 423, 441: .bg-success used for status dot.
- Line 300: .bg-primary.rounded-full used for tool invocation animation dot.

Replace all with data-testid selectors added in US-001.

Loader timing (lines 419-432): The test sets testServer.setSlow(3_000), fires a tool call, then checks for a loader. If system overhead consumes most of the 3s window, the loader may not be visible long enough. Fix: increase slow mode to 5-10 seconds, or use expect(locator).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 }) to poll for the loader appearance.

userStories.title: Update side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts to use data-testid selectors
userStories.description: As a test author, I want plugin toggle tests to use data-testid selectors for the status dot so that selector changes don't cause silent test breakage.
acceptanceCriteria: All .bg-success selectors (lines 72, 106) are replaced with data-testid selectors
Tests continue to pass with the updated selectors
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts

Lines 72, 106: .bg-success used for the green status dot indicating plugin is in 'ready' state. Replace with the data-testid selector added in US-001 (e.g., [data-testid='plugin-status-dot']).

This is a straightforward find-and-replace of the CSS class selector with the data-testid selector. Ensure the locator is scoped to the correct plugin card if there are multiple plugins visible.
